metadata.description: External sources research for Sutherland Number Theory Lecture 2 formalization
metadata.notes: Identifies sources beyond Mathlib for items not fully covered by Mathlib (Stage 2.3)

sources.name: Sutherland Number Theory Lecture 1 (FormalFrontier)
sources.description: Formalization of Lecture 1 of Sutherland's 18.785 Number Theory I. Contains DVR definitions (Def 1.10), valuation rings (Def 1.11), local rings (Def 1.12), residue fields (Def 1.13), Theorem 1.16 (7-way equivalence for DVRs including integrally closed noetherian local domain of dim 1), integral elements/closure (Defs 1.17, 1.19), and number fields/rings of integers (Def 1.26).
available_declarations: IsDiscreteValuationRing A : local PID, not a field
NumberField K : finite extension of Q
sources.notes: Pinned to commit 33c1d4d (last commit on lean4 v4.28.0 / mathlib v4.28.0, matching this project's toolchain). Later commits upgraded to v4.29.0-rc8 and are incompatible.

sources.name: Mathlib4
sources.description: Already a dependency (v4.28.0). Contains the bulk of the formalization infrastructure: localization, Dedekind domains, fractional ideals, class groups. The lean-forward/class-number project has been merged into mathlib. Covers most items fully; see mathlib-coverage.json for details.
sources.notes: Primary source for all undergraduate prerequisites (ext_commutative_rings, ext_integral_domains, ext_noetherian_rings, etc.)

sources.name: Atiyah & MacDonald, Introduction to Commutative Algebra
sources.description: Standard reference for commutative algebra. The lecture cites [1, Prop. 11.19] for contraction/extension properties and [1, Cor. 11.20] for the prime ideal correspondence (Theorem 2.1). These results are already in Mathlib, but the textbook provides detailed proof strategies if custom proofs are needed.

sources.name: Artin, Algebra (2nd ed.)
sources.description: The lecture cites [2, Prop. 3.5] for the tensor-localization equivalence S⁻¹M ≅ M ⊗_A S⁻¹A, and [2, Prop. 3.11] for contraction/extension and prime ideal correspondence. These are in Mathlib but the textbook is useful for proof strategy.

sources.name: lean-forward/class-number (ITP 2021)
sources.description: Original Lean 3 formalization of Dedekind domains and class groups of global fields, by Baanen, Dahmen, Narayanan, Nuccio. This work has been merged into mathlib (now mathlib4). Not a direct dependency, but documents proof strategies for Dedekind domain properties, fractional ideal operations, and class number finiteness that informed the mathlib API.
sources.notes: Lean 3 only. All relevant results now in mathlib4. Useful as documentation of proof strategies, not as a code dependency.

sources.name: Mathlib GaussianInt module
sources.description: Mathlib defines GaussianInt (Z[i]) and its ring structure. Example 2.21 needs the subring A = Z + 2iZ ⊂ Z[i], which is NOT in Mathlib and must be constructed from scratch.
sources.notes: Z[i] is available; the subring Z + 2iZ and the proof that I = 2Z[i] is not invertible as an A-ideal must be built custom. This is the only 'not_covered' item.

gap_analysis.description: Items with partial or no Mathlib coverage and how external sources help

02_04_Example.gap: Specific example: k[x] localized at (x-2) is a DVR. Needs assembly from Mathlib polynomial ring + localization + DVR infrastructure.
02_04_Example.external_help: Lecture 1 provides DVR definition and Theorem 1.16 characterization. Proof strategy from the blob file.

02_05_Example.gap: Specific example: Z_(p) is a DVR with p-adic valuation. Mathlib has padic infrastructure but assembly needed.
02_05_Example.external_help: Lecture 1 DVR definitions. Mathlib padic valuation.

02_06_Proposition.gap: General submodule intersection: M = ∩_p M_p. Mathlib has Dedekind domain version but not general integral domain version.
02_06_Proposition.external_help: Must be proved from scratch following the book's proof. No external Lean source found.

02_07_Corollary.gap: I = ∩_p I_p for ideals in integral domain. General version may need proof.

02_08_Example.gap: Z = ∩ Z_(p) in Q. Concrete instance of Corollary 2.7.

02_12_Remark.gap: Counterexamples: k[x,y] not Dedekind, Z[√(-13)] not UFD.
02_12_Remark.external_help: Folklore results. Must be constructed. Z[√(-13)] available via Mathlib Zsqrtd.

02_21_Example.gap: A = Z + 2iZ, I = 2Z[i] not invertible. Subring Z + 2iZ not in Mathlib.
02_21_Example.external_help: GaussianInt from Mathlib provides Z[i]. The subring and non-invertibility proof must be built from scratch.

02_24_Example.gap: DVR has trivial class group. Framework exists, specific result needs assembly.
02_24_Example.external_help: Lecture 1 DVR characterization. Mathlib class group machinery.

02_25_Remark.gap: Dedekind domain is UFD iff cl(A) trivial. Biconditional may need assembly.
02_25_Remark.external_help: Mathlib has both directions separately. Assembly needed.

conclusion: The primary external dependency is Lecture 1 (FormalFrontier), which provides DVR definitions, Theorem 1.16, and number field infrastructure used by 5 items. All undergraduate prerequisite material (commutative algebra, modules, etc.) is fully covered by Mathlib. The textbook references [1] (Atiyah-MacDonald) and [2] (Artin) provide proof strategies but their results are in Mathlib. The hardest gap is Example 2.21 (Z + 2iZ subring), which requires custom construction. Most partially-covered items need assembly of existing Mathlib components rather than fundamentally new mathematics.
